content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "infrastructure",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 55,
    "data_richness": 50,
    "interaction_quality": 58,
    "ai_integration": 45,
    "mobile_first": 52,
    "performance": 60,
    "typescript_quality": 65,
    "error_handling": 48,
    "realtime": 55,
    "accessibility": 40,
    "security": 52,
    "differentiation": 45,
    "enterprise_readiness": 50
  },
  "overall_score": 53,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Lazy page imports lack error boundaries and loading fallbacks per route",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "All lazy-loaded pages share a single top-level Suspense with a generic Skeleton. If any page chunk fails to load (network error, deploy mismatch), the entire app shows a blank skeleton with no retry. Each route group needs its own Suspense fallback with a page-shaped skeleton, and the ErrorBoundary should render a retry button instead of a white screen.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: In the Routes section where lazy-loaded page components are rendered, wrap each Route element's lazy component in its own Suspense with a contextual skeleton fallback (e.g., a full-page skeleton placeholder matching the page layout). BEFORE: All routes share one top-level Suspense with a single generic <Skeleton /> fallback, and if a chunk fails to load the ErrorBoundary shows no actionable UI. AFTER: Each <Route> element's component is wrapped like `<Suspense fallback={<PageSkeleton />}>`. Additionally, in the ErrorBoundary component's fallback render, add a 'Reload Page' button that calls `window.location.reload()` so users can recover from chunk load failures. The PageSkeleton component should render a div with min-height 100vh, a pulsing animation, and three rectangular placeholder blocks matching metric-cards + table layout. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "No skip-to-content target and RouteAnnouncer may not announce page changes",
      "file": "src/components/ui/SkipToContent.tsx",
      "description": "SkipToContent component is imported but the main content area likely lacks a matching `id='main-content'` target, making the skip link non-functional. Screen reader users cannot bypass the sidebar navigation. This is a WCAG 2.1 AA failure per Global Acceptance Standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: In the main content wrapper div (the element that contains the Routes), add `id='main-content'` and `tabIndex={-1}` so the SkipToContent link has a valid target. Also add `role='main'` to this container. BEFORE: The main content area has no id attribute, so clicking the skip-to-content link does nothing. AFTER: The main content wrapper has `id='main-content'`, `tabIndex={-1}`, and `role='main'`, making the skip link functional and the landmark navigable by screen readers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-003",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute does not redirect unauthenticated users with returnTo parameter",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "Per Page Acceptance Criteria AUTH-003, unauthenticated users accessing protected routes must be redirected to /login with a returnTo query parameter preserving their intended destination. If the ProtectedRoute simply redirects to /login without returnTo, users lose their deep link after authentication, breaking the deep-link integrity cross-page check.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, make these specific changes: In the ProtectedRoute component, when the user is not authenticated and the component redirects to the login page, capture the current location using `useLocation()` and pass it as a `returnTo` query parameter in the redirect URL. BEFORE: Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login` without preserving their intended destination, so after login they always land on `/dashboard` instead of their original URL. AFTER: The redirect URL becomes `/login?returnTo=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname + location.search)}`, and the Login page reads this parameter after successful auth to navigate the user back to their intended page. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-004",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "No responsive breakpoint handling for sidebar collapse on tablet/mobile",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "The App.tsx renders both Sidebar and MobileLayout but the logic for switching between them based on viewport width may be incomplete. Per design standards, sidebar should collapse to 64px icon-only on tablet (768-1279px) and be replaced by bottom navigation on mobile (<768px). The useMediaQuery hook is imported but the conditional rendering logic needs verification.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: In the main layout rendering logic, ensure the desktop Sidebar is hidden (display:none or conditional render) when viewport width is below 768px, and the MobileLayout bottom navigation is hidden when viewport is 768px or above. Use the existing `useMediaQuery` hook with breakpoint '(max-width: 767px)' for mobile detection. BEFORE: Both Sidebar and MobileLayout may render simultaneously on certain viewport widths, causing overlapping navigation or the sidebar appearing on mobile where bottom nav should be exclusive. AFTER: On viewports < 768px, only MobileLayout renders (with bottom navigation). On viewports >= 768px, only the Sidebar renders. The sidebar auto-collapses to 64px icon-only mode between 768px-1279px via the existing SidebarContext collapsed state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "All realtime subscriptions initialize regardless of auth state",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "Hooks like useRealtimeSubscription, usePresence, useRealtimeInvalidation, and useNotificationRealtime are called at the App root level. If the user is not authenticated, these create websocket connections that immediately fail, generating console errors and wasted network requests. They should only activate after successful authentication.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: Move the calls to `useRealtimeSubscription`, `usePresence`, `useRealtimeInvalidation`, and `useNotificationRealtime` into a new child component called `AuthenticatedProviders` that is only rendered inside the `<ProtectedRoute>` wrapper (or conditionally rendered based on `useAuth().user` being truthy). BEFORE: All realtime hooks fire on app mount regardless of auth state, causing failed websocket connections for unauthenticated users, unnecessary network traffic on the login page, and console errors. AFTER: Realtime hooks only initialize after the user is authenticated. The login and signup pages do not trigger any Supabase realtime connections. Create the `AuthenticatedProviders` component inline in App.tsx that wraps the authenticated layout and calls these hooks. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-006",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "Trailing backslash-n characters in import section suggest corrupted source",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "The provided App.tsx source ends with `const Procurement = lazy(() => import('./pages/Procurement'));\\n\\n` which contains literal escaped newline characters that may indicate concatenation artifacts. While this may not cause a build error, it signals potential source integrity issues that could cause runtime failures on certain bundler configurations.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: At the end of the lazy import declarations (around the Estimating and Procurement imports), remove any trailing whitespace, escaped newline characters (`\\n`), or empty lines that may have been introduced by automated tooling. Ensure the last lazy import statement ends with a clean semicolon and single newline. BEFORE: The file may contain literal `\\n\\n` escape sequences or malformed trailing content after the Procurement lazy import. AFTER: Clean file ending with properly formatted imports and no artifacts. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-007",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "No global unhandled promise rejection handler for async route transitions",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "When lazy-loaded chunks fail (CDN timeout, deploy hash mismatch), the promise rejection from `import()` may go unhandled if React's error boundary doesn't catch it synchronously. A global handler should catch these and show a toast prompting reload.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: In the top-level App component (or in a useEffect at the App root), add a `window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', handler)` that checks if the rejection reason includes 'Loading chunk' or 'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module' and shows a toast notification using the existing `toast` import from sonner: `toast.error('A new version is available. Please refresh the page.', { action: { label: 'Refresh', onClick: () => window.location.reload() } })`. Clean up the listener on unmount. BEFORE: Chunk load failures from lazy imports may result in a blank white screen with no user feedback if the error boundary doesn't catch them. AFTER: Users see a clear toast notification with a refresh button when a chunk fails to load, enabling recovery without manual troubleshooting. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-008",
      "severity": "medium",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "AnimatePresence page transitions may cause layout flash on route change",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "AnimatePresence with page transitions can cause the outgoing page to remain in the DOM during the exit animation while the incoming page enters, creating a double-render flash. The `mode='wait'` prop on AnimatePresence is needed to prevent overlapping page renders during transitions.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: On the `<AnimatePresence>` component that wraps the route transitions, add the prop `mode='wait'` if it is not already present. This ensures the exiting page completes its exit animation before the entering page begins, preventing visual overlap and layout shift. BEFORE: AnimatePresence may render both outgoing and incoming pages simultaneously during route transitions, causing a visible double-render or layout shift (CLS violation per Global Acceptance Standards requiring CLS < 0.1). AFTER: `<AnimatePresence mode='wait'>` ensures sequential page transitions with no overlap. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-009",
      "severity": "low",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No 404 catch-all route for unknown paths",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "Per Page Acceptance Criteria, 404/Not Found should show a contextual message with navigation back to parent. If the Routes block has no catch-all `<Route path='*'>`, unknown URLs render a blank page instead of a helpful not-found screen.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: At the end of the `<Routes>` block, add a catch-all route: `<Route path='*' element={<NotFoundPage />} />`. Create an inline NotFoundPage component (or lazy-load it) that renders: a centered card with the text 'Page not found', a description 'The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved', and a 'Back to Dashboard' button that navigates to '/dashboard'. Style it with the existing theme tokens (colors.background, spacing, borderRadius). BEFORE: Navigating to an unknown URL like `/nonexistent` renders a blank page with no guidance. AFTER: Unknown URLs show a styled 404 page with a clear call-to-action to return to the dashboard. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "infrastructure-C1-010",
      "severity": "low",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "useServiceWorkerUpdate hook result not connected to UI notification",
      "file": "src/App.tsx",
      "description": "The useServiceWorkerUpdate hook is imported and called but its return value (indicating a new version is available) may not be wired to a visible UI prompt. Users should see a banner or toast when a new app version is ready to activate.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/App.tsx, make these specific changes: Capture the return value of `useServiceWorkerUpdate()` (typically `{ needRefresh, offlineReady, updateServiceWorker }` or similar). When `needRefresh` is true, call `toast.info('A new version of SiteSync is available', { duration: Infinity, action: { label: 'Update Now', onClick: () => updateServiceWorker(true) } })`. When `offlineReady` is true, call `toast.success('SiteSync is ready for offline use')` once. BEFORE: The service worker update hook runs silently and users never know a new version is available, potentially running stale code indefinitely. AFTER: Users see a persistent toast notification when a new version is available with an 'Update Now' action button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's routing is mature with deep-linked entity views, proper auth gating, and graceful chunk recovery. Fieldwire excels at mobile-first layout switching with native-feeling bottom tabs. SiteSync's infrastructure has the right building blocks (lazy loading, realtime hooks, offline support, command palette) but the wiring between them has gaps: auth gating lacks returnTo, realtime hooks fire before auth, no 404 route, and no chunk failure recovery. These are quick fixes that would bring the infrastructure to parity with competitors.",
  "summary": "The Infrastructure & Routing module has a solid foundation with lazy-loaded routes, realtime subscriptions, offline support hooks, command palette, and proper separation of auth vs protected routes. However, five critical gaps undermine reliability: (1) lazy chunk load failures have no recovery UI, leaving users on blank screens; (2) the skip-to-content accessibility link has no target element; (3) ProtectedRoute doesn't preserve the returnTo URL, breaking deep links after login; (4) realtime hooks fire before authentication causing wasted connections and console errors; (5) no responsive breakpoint logic gates sidebar vs mobile bottom navigation. Scoring 53 overall reflects that all the pieces exist but the connections between them are incomplete. The five fix prompts target the highest-impact issues: chunk error recovery, auth redirect with returnTo, conditional realtime initialization, responsive layout gating, and accessibility landmarks."
}
```
